summary: Daniel Okafor led a Q1 business review with Yuki Tanaka of Pineridge, joined by Kevin O'Brien and Ananya Sharma. Yuki expressed strong overall satisfaction with the Aegis platform, particularly highlighting Aegis Detect's improved signal-to-noise ratio and a successful DR drill using Aegis Protect. A significant portion of the call focused on the upcoming Aegis Comply v2 release, which Yuki found compelling for their SOC 2 readiness needs and indicated could strengthen the ROI case for renewal. The call concluded with a positive renewal outlook, with Yuki requesting a separate commercial discussion in mid-April after reviewing the Comply v2 details.
actionItems: Ananya Sharma: Create a tiered restore playbook tailored to Pineridge's architecture based on DR drill learnings
Daniel Okafor: Send customized ROI template for Aegis Comply v2 that Yuki can present to CFO and leadership
Daniel Okafor: Add Yuki Tanaka to the Aegis Comply v2 early access communication list ahead of April 7th GA
Daniel Okafor: Schedule commercial renewal follow-up call with Yuki for the week of April 13th

keyMoments.text: Yuki praised Aegis Detect for dramatically improving signal-to-noise ratio, calling it night and day compared to manual log correlation
keyMoments.speaker: Yuki Tanaka

keyMoments.text: Yuki reported a successful DR drill with a two-hour RTO, meeting engineering leadership expectations
keyMoments.speaker: Yuki Tanaka

keyMoments.text: Yuki expressed genuine excitement about on-demand SOC 2 reporting in Comply v2, noting it changes her thinking on the renewal value proposition
keyMoments.speaker: Yuki Tanaka

keyMoments.text: Yuki confirmed intent to renew but flagged tighter budget and requested a separate commercial discussion focused on pricing structure
keyMoments.speaker: Yuki Tanaka
